name: Universal File Access
author: itz-mune
description: Lets RAGdoll search for files on your computer using natural language.
long_description: Searches your Documents, Desktop, Downloads and other standard directories using an indexed, fuzzy-matched search engine. Builds a local file index on first install (under 30 s for most systems) and keeps it current with a live file-system watcher. Results are shown instantly — enable 'Ask permission' in settings to add a confirmation step (critical system paths always require approval).

config_fields.label: Search give-up condition
config_fields.help: What triggers RAGdoll to stop searching and ask you for more context.

config_fields.label: Threshold value
config_fields.help: Stop searching after this many files, seconds, or characters (depending on the condition above).

config_fields.label: Index rebuild interval (hours)
config_fields.help: How often to fully rebuild the file index. Lower = more current, higher = less CPU on startup.

config_fields.label: Include hidden files in search
config_fields.help: Include files beginning with . (Unix) or marked as hidden (Windows).

config_fields.label: Minimum match score (0–100)
config_fields.help: Files scoring below this threshold are excluded. Higher = stricter matching.

config_fields.label: Ask permission before showing results
config_fields.help: When on, shows a confirmation dialog before listing found files. Critical system-path files always require permission regardless of this setting.

actions.label: Rebuild file index
actions.description: Force a full rescan of your directories. Takes up to 30 seconds.

actions.label: Clear index
actions.description: Delete the cached file index from disk.
actions.confirm_message: This deletes the file index cache. RAGdoll will rebuild it automatically on the next search.

changelog.1.0.0: Initial release — indexed file search with live watchdog and permission popup.
